# nuxt-directus-sdk

> A Nuxt module for Directus with built-in authentication, realtime, file management, type generation, and visual editor support.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rolleyio/nuxt-directus-sdk
- Docs: https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io

## Overview

Production-ready Nuxt module for Directus CMS. Handles session-based authentication, realtime WebSocket subscriptions, file management, type generation from the Directus schema, visual editor integration, and permissions/rules sync. Sensible defaults out of the box; opt-in advanced configuration when you need it.

## Guides

- [Getting Started](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/getting-started) — Installation, Directus backend configuration, next steps
- [Authentication](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/authentication) — Session-based auth, SSO/OAuth, protected routes, user management
- [Realtime & WebSockets](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/realtime) — Live subscriptions, auth modes, dev proxy
- [File Management](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/files) — Uploads, transformations, `@nuxt/image` integration
- [Visual Editor](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/visual-editor) — `DirectusVisualEditor` component, iframe detection, preview mode
- [Type Generation](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/type-generation) — Auto-generated types, prefix, filtering with `include` / `exclude`
- [Dev Proxy](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/dev-proxy) — CORS-free local development, split URLs for Docker/K8s
- [Server-Side Utilities](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/server-side) — Server routes, admin operations, token auth

## Rules DSL

- [Defining Rules](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules) — Code-first permissions and policies
- [Testing Rules](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules-testing) — Unit-test access-control decisions
- [Sync & CLI](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules-sync) — Pull, diff, and push rules

## CLI

- [Overview](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli) — Connection settings, exit codes
- [`generate-types`](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli/generate-types) — Generate TypeScript types from a Directus schema
- [`rules:*`](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli/rules) — Pull, push, and diff Directus permissions/policies/roles

## API Reference

- [Module Options](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/module) — `directus.*` configuration options
- [Environment Variables](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/env) — `DIRECTUS_URL`, `DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN`, etc.
- [Directus Server Configuration](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/server) — CORS, cookies, and other backend settings needed for cross-domain setups
- [Composables](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/composables) — `useDirectus`, `useDirectusAuth`, `useDirectusStorage`, file helpers

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add -D nuxt-directus-sdk
```

```typescript
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['nuxt-directus-sdk'],
  directus: {
    url: process.env.DIRECTUS_URL,
  },
})
```

```dotenv
# .env
DIRECTUS_URL=https://your-directus.com
DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN=your-admin-token
```

## Requirements

- Nuxt 4.0+
- Directus v11.16.0+ (required by `@directus/visual-editing` v2 and `@directus/sdk` v21)

## SDK access

The module auto-imports every function exported by `@directus/sdk` at build time, so you can call `readItems`, `createItem`, `aggregate`, `customEndpoint`, etc. directly without importing. A small exclusion list covers client construction (`createDirectus`, `rest`, `realtime`, `staticToken`, `authentication`), auth internals (`auth`, `getAuthEndpoint`, `memoryStorage`), and GraphQL (`graphql`, `readGraphqlSdl`) — see the [Composables page](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/composables#functions-excluded-from-auto-import) for why each is excluded and how to import them manually when needed.

The auto-import set tracks whichever version of `@directus/sdk` the user has installed, so new SDK functions are available automatically on upgrade. Disable or customise via `directus.autoImportSdk` in config.

## Type generation

Types are generated from the Directus schema at build time and made available globally (`DirectusSchema`, `CollectionNames`, one interface per collection). Enabled by default when `DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN` is set.

Options: `prefix` to namespace custom collection types, `include` / `exclude` to narrow what's emitted (references to missing collections collapse to `string` / `string[]`), `expandReferences` to auto-pull referenced collections when using `include`, and `verbose` for per-reference rewrite logs. See the [Type Generation guide](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/type-generation) for the full story.

## Breaking changes in 6.0

- `@directus/sdk` peer dep bumped to `>=21`
- `@directus/types` peer dep bumped to `>=15`
- `@directus/visual-editing` peer dep bumped to `>=2.0` (requires Directus v11.16.0+)
- `DirectusAddButton` and `DirectusEditButton` components removed; use `useDirectusVisualEditor()` or `@directus/visual-editing`'s `apply()` directly for custom UI
